reason: User observed that several Wave70 masks passed generated-output QA despite questionable anatomical alignment. This audit separates generated-output stability from semantic mask alignment.
policy: Generated-output stable does not equal mask-alignment pass. Final completion requires semantic mask alignment, protected-neighbor pass, generated-output stability, and target-runtime proof.

findings: Retro audit from user-supplied overlay review found the expression-region mask is too broad for the named brows/eyes/cheeks/mouth expression target.
Generated-output geometry evidence may remain useful, but semantic mask alignment must be revised before completion or certification.
Future expression-region masks must separate target expression features from broad face-identity coverage.
Retro audit classified the forehead overlay as acceptable with notes for the named forehead-skin target.
Completion still requires target-runtime proof before final certification.
Retro audit from user-supplied overlay review found the cheek masks too loose/polygonal for precise left/right cheek-skin coverage.
Generated output may remain safe at low denoise, but cheek semantic alignment needs revision before row completion.
Future cheek masks must better follow cheek anatomy while avoiding protected eyes, nose, mouth, hairline, and jawline.
Retro audit found the jaw/chin mask does not follow the jawline/chin contour tightly enough for the named target.
Generated output may remain safe, but mask alignment must be revised before completion or certification.
Future jaw/chin masks must avoid mouth, broad neck, hair, and clothing while tracing the actual contour.
Retro audit found the broad skin-continuity mask includes an overextended neck/chest/clothing-shaped region risk.
Generated output safety at low denoise does not prove the global visible-skin mask is semantically correct.
Future skin-continuity masks must exclude clothing and background and distinguish face/neck/chest skin boundaries.
Retro policy update did not receive enough explicit overlay review evidence to certify semantic alignment for the both-eyes full-region mask.
Generated-output stability may remain recorded, but semantic mask-alignment must be re-reviewed under the new protocol before completion.
Retro audit from user-supplied overlay review found the single-eye mask too loose/shifted for precise full-eye semantic coverage.
Generated output may remain safe, but semantic alignment must be revised before completion or certification.
Future single-eye masks must align to the visible eye aperture/associated full-eye region and avoid unrelated skin or brow coverage.
Retro audit from user-supplied overlay review found the single-eye mask too loose/shifted for precise full-eye semantic coverage.
Generated output may remain safe, but semantic alignment must be revised before completion or certification.
Future single-eye masks must align to the visible eye aperture/associated full-eye region and avoid unrelated skin or brow coverage.
Retro audit from user-supplied overlay review found the mask covers mostly iris/pupil cores and does not honestly represent pupils_iris_sclera.
The sclera component is not adequately covered, so semantic mask alignment fails even if low-denoise generated output was stable.
Future pupils/iris/sclera masks must explicitly include the visible sclera while protecting eyelids, eyelashes, catchlights, and surrounding skin.
Retro audit from user-supplied overlay review found the eyelid mask is still questionable/too loose for upper/lower eyelid semantic alignment.
Generated output may remain safe, but mask alignment needs revision before completion or certification.
Future eyelid masks must follow actual lid folds/bands while avoiding iris, sclera, lashes, and broad skin.
Retro audit classified the eyelash mask as acceptable with notes: it hugs visible lash-line bands and avoids broad protected regions.
Completion still requires target-runtime proof before final certification.
Retro audit classified the under-eye mask as acceptable with notes: it sits below the lower lids and avoids eye cores, broad cheeks, nose, and background.
Completion still requires target-runtime proof before final certification.
